user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Angelica Le","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIOWBMa7jVofIayjpnICgpnxr9edArDUjsP5bGPjhHs-B9ToL8l=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Overall: 3.9/5 [dine in]

Cafe Creme is conveniently located right next to the library, so it attracts a lot of people who come to study. The space is huge and unique, with art on display that you can buy. Menus are left at the front for you to review, and when you are ready to order you can go up to the cashier. They give you a table number so they know who to bring the food out to but drinks are picked up at the counter by the cashier. They have self serve water and to go box stations which are convenient, and the restroom code is printed on the receipt.

Nova Scotia Crepe ($16) + Fried Egg ($2): Smoked salmon, honey mustard, spinach, tomato, onion, capers. It was good with the fried egg on top but tasted a bit plain without it. I prefer smoked salmon on a bagel with cream cheese rather than in a crepe.

Saigon Crepe ($16) + Fried Egg ($2): Skirt steak, potato, cheddar, onion, spinach, soy sauce. This crepe is not available after 8 PM. My husband got his without cheddar, so I thought it was lacking flavor. However, my husband liked his crepe and thought it was better than he expected.

Brew & Brew is a true hipster spot, not just because of the carefully curated aesthetic but because the people who come here actually create the atmosphere. It’s an effortlessly cool, craft-focused café that feels genuinely local.

The coffee is excellent—well-balanced, smooth, and clearly made with attention to detail. The menu extends beyond coffee, offering breakfast, brunch, and lunch options. We ordered the bagel with lox, which was fresh, well-assembled, and full of flavor. A simple dish, but done right.

The location is interesting—it’s near downtown but not quite downtown, giving it a slightly tucked-away, neighborhood feel. The space is well-designed, with indoor seating, a bar counter, and outdoor tables for those who want to enjoy the fresh air.

One of the standout features of Brew & Brew is that it transitions seamlessly from a coffee shop to a bar. After lunch, you can grab a beer, wine, or cocktail, making it a great all-day spot, whether you need a morning pick-me-up or an evening wind-down.

The staff is friendly, and the service is smooth, even when the place is busy. If you appreciate well-made coffee, a laid-back but lively vibe, and a place where you can seamlessly move from work mode to social mode, Brew & Brew is one of the best choices in the Went here yesterday excited to try the Manchild Matcha. I get there, place my order and the barista preparing the drinks immediately goes, “ugh another Manchild Matcha” rather loudly. I pay no mind to it, surely he has prepared many of them this past weekend and 2 ladies ahead of me had just ordered them.
While my drink is being prepped, I ask what the code to the restroom was to this same barista. At first I thought he didn’t hear me as he didn’t even look in my direction or acknowledge the question. He quietly groans out the code to the bathroom (1003, in case someone wants to save themselves from this delight) - ok, cool. I was clearly the bane of this man’s existence today.
I get my matcha, and there’s no strawberry jam at the bottom. I give it a good mix but there’s none. The 2 ladies before me had beautiful Manchild Matchas exactly how Mañana had advertised, with jam at the bottom. I kindly let him know there’s no jam at the bottom and he squirted a bit at the top. Silly me, thinking he’d realized that he forgot a step (happens to all of us, unless you’re him of course) but he just squirts some at the top. The matcha was terrible though so I threw it out.

I’m a frequent flyer at Good Grief run club on Monday mornings, so I love that the crew knows my order before I even step up to the counter. 🥹 I’m also a creature of habit (iced s’mores latte no matter the weather) so when I was encouraged to try a new drink (!) [{"Name":"Dylan T","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXHBi7gPCNPc2sPh4NZYfCo5_3Nj49M_oVB8sJzHeegzaWESjIlYQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"TL;DR: One of my favorite coffee shops that I have been to! I will definitely be back.

Coffee:
I am not sure if this is a seasonal drink, but I ordered the Chinese coffee with tonic and orange (under the New Year, New Sips section). The orange and cinnamon flavor are quite strong, which I really enjoyed. I got to watch them torch a cinnamon stick and then put it into my drink which was so cool.

Service:
The baristas here seem so friendly! I was given a very thorough explanation of the drink that I was ordering, and I felt good about the decision I made. Everyone in the coffee shop seemed very cheery :)

Ambiance:
+ I absolutely love the vibe here. Warm light streams in through the windows. The dark green walls with accents of red from the curtains feels cozy. It is a very laid back spot to sip on a coffee, chat with some friends, or lock in to work.
+ This is also a very clean restroom and probably one of the largest ones I have ever seen in a restaurant or coffee shop before? There is just a single room, but it is very spacious and comfortable if you need to use the can.
+ There is also free WiFi and plenty of other nice spots (coffee, food, shops) along this I will definitely be back.

Coffee:
I am not sure if this is a seasonal drink, but I ordered the Chinese coffee with tonic and orange (under the New Year, New Sips section). The orange and cinnamon flavor are quite strong, which I really enjoyed. I got to watch them torch a cinnamon stick and then put it into my drink which was so cool.

Service:
The baristas here seem so friendly! I was given a very thorough explanation of the drink that I was ordering, and I felt good about the decision I made. Everyone in the coffee shop seemed very cheery :)

Ambiance:
+ I absolutely love the vibe here. Warm light streams in through the windows. The dark green walls with accents of red from the curtains feels cozy. It is a very laid back spot to sip on a coffee, chat with some friends, or lock in to work.
